old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-8zcp","title":"gpsr_forward: neighbor coords only check NaN/inf, not range","description":"File: python/src/lichen/routing/router.py:794-799

Neighbor coordinates are only validated for NaN/inf:

```python
for neighbor, coords in self.neighbor_coords.items():
    n_lat, n_lon = coords
    if math.isnan(n_lat) or math.isnan(n_lon) or math.isinf(n_lat) or math.isinf(n_lon):
        logger.warning(...)
        continue
    d = _haversine(coords, dst_coords)
```

But destination coordinates are also validated for range (lines 786-788):
```python
if not (-90 <= lat <= 90 and -180 <= lon <= 180):
    logger.warning("gpsr: invalid dst_coords (%s, %s)", lat, lon)
    return None
```

WHY IT'S A BUG: Inconsistent validation between dst_coords and neighbor_coords:
- dst_coords: NaN, inf, null island, AND range checked
- neighbor_coords: only NaN and inf checked

A neighbor with coords (999, 999) would pass validation and produce incorrect haversine distances (math.radians would still work, but results would be meaningless).

IMPACT: Incorrect GPSR routing decisions when neighbors have out-of-range coordinates.

FIX: Apply the same validation to neighbor coords:
```python
if not (-90 <= n_lat <= 90 and -180 <= n_lon <= 180):
    logger.warning("gpsr: neighbor %s has out-of-range coords", neighbor)
    continue
```","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:25:00.428532Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:25:00.428578Z"}

    logger.warning("gpsr: neighbor %s has out-of-range coords", neighbor)
    continue
